title: varronia glandulosa
intro: Varronia glandulosa, also known as the tree bush or wild jasmine, is a plant species in the family Boraginaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits associated with Varronia glandulosa include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
